title: AAC Clyde Space wins ESA contract to complete maritime-monitoring constellation
summary: AAC Clyde Space secured a €10.9 million contract from the European Space Agency to develop and demonstrate VHF Data Exchange System (VDES) satellites for maritime monitoring. This contract aims to complete the constellation for enhanced maritime communication and tracking.

title: Vandenberg offers new launch site for small and medium rockets
summary: The U.S. Space Force is offering a new launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base for small and medium rockets. This initiative aims to attract potential users for the facility.

title: Understanding what’s next for orbital data centers
summary: SpaceNews discusses the future of orbital data centers in a recent event, exploring industry interest and technological advancements. Industry leaders and analysts share insights on the driving factors behind this emerging space technology.

title: Senate NDAA backs plan to fold SDA, Space RCO into Space Force
summary: The Senate NDAA proposes integrating the Space Development Agency (SDA) and Space Rapid Capabilities Office (Space RCO) into the Space Force by eliminating their separate statutory requirements. This move aligns with broader Pentagon procurement reforms outlined in the 2027 defense bill.

title: ESA astronaut assignment on Artemis 3 part of negotiations on revised Artemis roles
summary: ESA's astronaut assignment to Artemis 3 is part of ongoing negotiations to redefine roles in the Artemis program, aiming to secure European astronauts' participation in lunar missions.

title: HyImpulse signs letter of intent with Oman’s spaceport
summary: HyImpulse, a German small launch provider, signed a letter of intent with Oman's Etlaq Spaceport to explore future launch activities and potential operational presence. The agreement aims to facilitate commercial space launches from Oman.

title: Tilebox Launches Verifiable AI Workflows for Satellite Data
summary: Tilebox introduced infrastructure for verifiable AI workflows on Earth observation satellite data, enabling teams to use agents through governed and repeatable processes.

title: K2 Space, Rocket Lab win key supplier roles in Space Force satcom program
summary: K2 Space and Rocket Lab have been selected as key suppliers for spacecraft buses to SES and Viasat under the U.S. Space Force's next-generation protected satcom program. This collaboration aims to enhance military satellite communications capabilities.

title: GEO cancellations complicate space insurance recovery
summary: The cancellation of three geostationary satellites has created challenges for space insurers seeking financial recovery. SES disclosed the cancellations, impacting insurers' expectations of recovering losses from recent claims.

title: Open Cosmos seeks deadline extension for broadband constellation
summary: Open Cosmos, a British small satellite specialist, is requesting an extension for deploying its sovereign broadband constellation due to launch delays. The company faces challenges in meeting the original timeline for the European broadband network.

title: SpaceX sends 24 Starlink satellites into orbit as market awaits IPO launch (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 24 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket. The mission occurred amid anticipation for the company's upcoming IPO on the NASDAQ.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers 'galaxy-killing' wind that may explain why some early galaxies lived fast and died young
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) and ALMA have discovered a 'galaxy-killing' wind driven by cosmic mergers, which may explain why massive early galaxies stopped forming stars prematurely. This finding sheds light on the rapid demise of star formation in these ancient galaxies.

title: Don't miss this beautiful 3-planet parade after sunset on June 12 — it won't last long
summary: On June 12, Mercury, Venus, and Jupiter will form a rare three-planet alignment visible low in the western sky after sunset. This brief celestial event offers a stunning visual display for stargazers.

title: 'Star City''s Agnes O'Casey and Anna Maxwell Martin spill the tea on Soviet severity, fighting for trousers, and furry hats
summary: The news discusses Agnes O'Casey and Anna Maxwell Martin's portrayal of Soviet-era challenges in 'Star City'. The conversation highlights themes of gender roles and resilience in a harsh environment.

title: Grab our best telescope for beginners with $100 off the Celestron NexStar 4SE
summary: Celestron's NexStar 4SE telescope, a beginner-friendly model with a 4-inch aperture and go-to technology, is on sale with a $100 discount on Amazon.

title: Astronauts watch from space as ice splinters from a glacier | Space photo of the day for June 11, 2026
summary: Astronauts observe ice breaking off from a glacier in the Southern Patagonia Icefield from space. The changes in the glacier are visible in a space photo dated June 11, 2026.

title: 'Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2' upgrades the sci-fi action shooter to the state-of-the-badass art
summary: Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 is a sci-fi action shooter game that has been upgraded with enhanced features. The preview was guided by game director Chris Cross.

title: Why do stars appear different colors in the night sky?
summary: Stars appear in different colors due to variations in their temperature and composition, with blue and white stars being hotter and red stars cooler. These colors provide insights into the stars' age, size, and life cycle.

title: Elon Musk wants to put 1 million AI satellites in space. Here's how SpaceX could do it
summary: Elon Musk announced SpaceX's plan to deploy 1 million AI satellites in space, detailing how this could be achieved ahead of a potential IPO expected to make him a trillionaire.

title: Don't miss out on Star Trek: Strange New Worlds season 4 — stream it from anywhere at warp speed using this Norton VPN deal, now up to 70% off
summary: A Norton VPN advertisement promotes streaming Star Trek: Strange New Worlds season 4 with up to 70% off. Norton is highlighted as a top VPN service for online security and convenience.

title: NASA Award Boosts Space Technology Research Capabilities
summary: NASA opened a new funding opportunity called M-STAR to accelerate academic research and technology development. The application window for the Minority University Research and Education Project Space Technology Artemis Research closes on August 11.

title: NASA’s Chandra Discovers Possible Supernova Remnant in Galactic Center
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory may have discovered a supernova remnant in the Galactic Center. The findings were published in The Astrophysical Journal.

title: I Am Artemis: Elkin Norena
summary: Elkin Norena, NASA's Space Launch System Program resident management officer, has overseen more than a dozen space shuttle missions. These missions contributed to building the International Space Station and other significant achievements.

title: NASA Robotic Tech Demo Will Advance Prototype Gamma-Ray Detectors
summary: NASA is advancing prototype gamma-ray detectors through a robotic arm demonstration on its upcoming Fly Foundational Robots mission. The new gamma-ray sensor technology aims to enhance scientific capabilities in space exploration.

title: Soccer Meets Space Science
summary: A soccer ball was tested on the International Space Station to study how internal mass affects motion in microgravity. The findings could improve the design of embedded technologies in soccer balls.

title: NASA’s Career Technical Education Day Highlights Technical Careers
summary: NASA hosted Career Technical Education Day to highlight technical careers and cultivate the next generation of skilled workers. The event emphasized the critical role of technicians in advancing NASA's research and missions.

title: NASA, USGS Scientists Go Rock Hounding in California’s High Desert
summary: NASA and USGS scientists explored California's Mojave Desert to investigate a sensor-detected 'fingerprint' of topaz, which could indicate valuable geological formations. The team used rock picks and hand lenses to study the mineral, aiming to uncover insights beyond gemstones.

title: NASA to Preview Katalyst Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
summary: NASA is preparing to preview the Katalyst Space mission, which aims to boost the orbit of the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory using a robotic servicing spacecraft called LINK. The mission aims to extend the spacecraft's science mission lifespan.

title: Air Pollution’s Daily Pulse Over the Northeast
summary: The TEMPO mission tracked daily nitrogen dioxide levels contributing to afternoon ozone formation along the New York–Washington corridor in May 2026. This data highlighted air pollution patterns in the Northeast.

title: Curiosity Blog: Sols 4913-4919: Planetary explorers, freewheeling to the Yardang unit!
summary: The Curiosity rover is transitioning to a new science campaign focused on the Yardang unit, continuing its exploration of Mars. The blog details the rover's activities and planning over sols 4913-4919.

title: Japan’s H3 to return to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket is set to return to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant step for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly on the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This crewed mission is a key step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has started cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will see launches from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink, including three Starlink missions. Flights are scheduled from California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn rocket anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability capabilities.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 is evaluating damage and future risks after an anomaly. The Eastern Range operations are under review to assess potential dangers.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparations for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The provided news snippet is incomplete and lacks sufficient context to extract meaningful information.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute testing process for a space mission, likely involving extreme conditions to ensure reliability. This bake-out procedure is essential for validating the parachute's performance before deployment.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will endure a six-minute descent to slow the rover for a safe landing on Mars in 2028.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox launched an update to enhance AI agents' capabilities in analyzing geospatial data. The update aims to improve the effectiveness of AI-driven geospatial analytics.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on the accuracy of SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its IPO, though they agree the space industry is experiencing significant growth.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC satellite, entering the orbital data center market. This marks a significant step for the company in the space industry.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital secures $5 million in pre-seed funding to develop its ODC constellation, which uses smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground contact.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to launch at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing restrictions to encourage faster deployment.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA announced that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lunar lander for the Artemis III mission's Earth orbit test in 2027, paving the way for future Moon landings. The event marked a key step in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has chosen Arkadia Space to supply a propulsion system for its 2027 satellite mission. The partnership marks a significant step in advancing satellite technology.

title: ICEYE, Isar Aerospace Announce Significant Funding Rounds
summary: ICEYE, a Finnish Earth observation company, raised €450M ($520M) in a Series F funding round at a €10B ($12B) valuation. Isar Aerospace was also mentioned in the funding announcement.

title: Quantum Space To Go Public Via SPAC
summary: Quantum Space plans to go public through a SPAC merger to accelerate growth and access capital. The company emphasizes the need for speed in scaling its operations.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop and scale Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) spacecraft. The company plans to launch its first VLEO-capable NEO-1 satellite in 2028.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, leveraging investor confidence in Elon Musk and expectations of massive AI growth. The company, which has operated privately for 24 years, aims to go public amid strong market interest.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has exited stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer. The company aims to build this network to support future space missions.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge secured £10 million in new investment from the European Space Agency and UK Space Agency. The funding is part of ESA's General Support Technology Programme, supported by UKSA.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their collaboration in space defense following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The partnership aims to enhance mutual security interests in the space domain.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation data company, is expanding its operations into Brazil, a key market in Latin America. The move is driven by Brazil's climate challenges and economic growth.

title: Airbus to Lead German Consortium for Sovereign ISR Capability
summary: Airbus is leading a German consortium to develop a sovereign intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) solution. The announcement was made during the Berlin Air Show, involving multiple German companies.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT to Purchase Avanti’s HYLAS 3 Satellite
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT will acquire Avanti's HYLAS 3 satellite currently in orbit and subject it to in-orbit testing. The deal expands Sky Perfect JSAT's satellite fleet.

title: LeoLabs Deploys First Mobile Tracking Radar
summary: LeoLabs has deployed its first mobile space domain awareness tracking radar in the Asia-Pacific region, housed in a shipping container. The new system, named Scout-S, marks a significant expansion of its space tracking capabilities.

title: 40 People Who Shaped the Satellite Industry: Part One
summary: Via Satellite celebrates its 40-year anniversary in 2026 by highlighting 40 influential people who have shaped the satellite industry. The feature honors both past and present visionaries in the field.

title: Space Force Eyes Nearly $1.5B for Space Data Network Backbone in Future Reconciliation Bill
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to request nearly $1.5 billion for the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone in a future fiscal 2027 reconciliation bill. This funding aims to enhance the military's space data infrastructure.
